_meta.note: LOCAL VA Tidewater batch 2026-06-04 19:19 — Virginia Beach Council 5-pack: D1 Hutcheson + D10 Rouse + D9 Schulman + D3 Berlucchi + D5 Wilson (Vice Mayor). rick-west DEFERRED 10TH-consecutive (Chesapeake Mayor — slug-collision with OK State Rep Rick West remains; refine-records.py:366 by_slug last-wins; NOT re-escalating). MAJOR FINDING — JAN 6 2020 2A 'ROUSE' DISAMBIGUATION: prior batch dossiers attributed the NO vote to 'Rouse'; that was AARON ROUSE (then VB at-large, now state Sen VA-22 D) — NOT Jennifer Rouse (his wife, took office Jan 2023 on the new D10 seat). Henley + Jones + Aaron Rouse + Wooten voted NO; Jennifer Rouse never voted on a 2A resolution. CROSS-BATCH CORRECTION — BERLUCCHI APPOINTMENT: he was appointed May 7 2019 to fill SHANNON KANE's Rose Hall seat (NOT Sessoms's). Sessoms resigned the mayoralty in 2018 and was succeeded by Dyer (covered in 18:30 batch). CROSS-BATCH CORRECTION — WILSON SPOUSE: Rosemary Wilson is NOT married to former Mayor Will Sessoms. She is a widow (husband died of rare cancer ~2017-2019); founded a $1.2M cancer-research fundraising legacy in his memory. HETERODOX BATCH: 4 of 5 records here are heterodox: (a) Hutcheson R-affiliated but VBEA/Teamsters/IAFF labor-coalition endorsed + voted YES on FY26 hike AND April 2024 collective-bargaining resolution; (b) Jennifer Rouse D-network independent + LONE NO on the April 16 2026 oceanfront curfew (10-1, post-mass-shooting) with explicit pro-engagement anti-enforcement framing — strongest single signal in her record; (c) Schulman PVG-listed, NOT on RPVB roster, beat conservative John Moss in 2022, voted YES on FY26 hike, name correction Joashua F. 'Joash' Schulman (slug retained for stability); (d) Berlucchi first openly gay VBCC member, ex-Hampton Roads Pride pres, sponsored Oct 2019 GA conversion-therapy ban resolution, Northam + Youngkin dual-appointee LGBTQ+ Advisory Board — but voted YES Jan 2020 2A resolution AND has PBA/FOP/Sheriff Holcomb/ex-Chief Jacocks endorsements (classic split-issue R-aligned but socially-progressive); (e) Wilson VB Vice Mayor 30-year career, voted YES Jan 2020 2A AND YES FY26 hike, no recorded dissent on 5+ years of Pride proclamations. STRUCTURAL SUMMARY OF VB COUNCIL POST-FY26: economic_stewardship.2 is essentially uniformly FALSE for everyone except Henley (the sole 10-1 NO), making it the cleanest cross-cutting fiscal-discipline indicator in Tidewater.

profile.background: Virginia Beach City Council, District 1 (Kempsville; officially nonpartisan). Appears on Republican Party of Virginia Beach 'My Elected Representatives' page (rpvb.org/elected-officials) but without the explicit '(R)' label other R councilmembers carry there — soft R-affiliation; ran labor-coalition campaign. Retired Virginia Beach Fire Chief — 35 yrs with VBFD, final 5 as Chief; retired Dec 1, 2022. BA Hampton University; MA Homeland Security from Naval Postgraduate School (Center for Homeland Defense and Security). Won D1 special election Jan 9, 2024 (defeated Napier + Gladney), sworn in Jan 16, 2024 to complete unexpired Chris Taylor term. Won full 4-yr term Nov 5, 2024 vs Ryan Gilliam, 61%-39%. Current term ends Dec 31, 2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. 2024 endorsement coalition included IAFF Local 2924 (firefighters), VBEA (teachers union), Teamsters, Coastal VA Building Industry Assoc., and PBA. No publicly stated church affiliation or profession of Christian faith located in any source searched.

hutch-hutcheson.notes_set: [2026-06-04 19:19 RESOLUTE refinement] 30 LOCAL cells scored as 1T / 1F / 28 NULL = 50% (2 scored cells). SOFT TRUE public_justice q0 — career firefighter / Fire Chief; campaign platform pillar #1 is 'Continuing Public Safety Excellence' and 'champion[ing] initiatives that keep Fire, EMS, Police, E911, and Emergency Management at the forefront'; PBA endorsement reinforces. Clears HIGH BAR for affirmative advocacy via explicit platform commitment (not just budget vote). HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2 — voted YES with 10-1 majority on FY26 budget May 13, 2025 (meals tax 5.5%→6%, stormwater + boat-reg hikes, library cuts) AND publicly defended it: 'There's a lot of parts that go into it. We have to take care of everybody. That's our job. Our roads, our public safety, our city employees. You got to pay for all of that, and none of the projects are going down. Nothing's getting cheaper.' Affirmative sausage-making defense of the regressive bundle Henley alone opposed. NULL everywhere else. NOT seated for the Jan 6, 2020 2A Constitutional City vote (took office Jan 16, 2024 — three years after). HETERODOX CATCHES: (1) Labor-coalition endorsement stack VBEA + IAFF + Teamsters + PBA — public-sector-union backed campaign; voted YES on the April 30, 2024 5-5-1 collective-bargaining resolution (failed on Ross-Hammond abstention) alongside Wooten/Rouse/Schulman/Remick, defending the ~$1M annual cost as offset by retention savings; (2) RPVB lists him without the '(R)' label other Republicans carry — claims the seat but the official does not campaign openly as R. Recommend revisit after any 2025-2026 public-safety / police-funding statement that would convert q0 from SOFT to HARD.

profile.background: Virginia Beach City Council, District 10 (new single-member district under post-VRA 10-1 system; officially nonpartisan). Jennifer Valentine Rouse — born-and-raised VB; Virginia Wesleyan College 2008 (honors); VCU MS; L. Douglas Wilder Graduate Scholar Fellow. Assistant Professor of Sociology + Department Chair of Social Sciences and Education Pathway at Tidewater Community College. VPAP labels her 'Independent Candidate'; not on RPVB roster; not D-Committee-endorsed; FUNCTIONALLY D-leaning Independent. Wife of state Sen. AARON ROUSE (D-VA Senate District 22; former VB at-large councilmember 2018-2023). Won D10 Nov 8, 2022 (~54%) vs Heidi Daniels; sworn in Jan 1, 2023. Term ends Dec 31, 2026 — ON the Nov 2026 ballot. CRITICAL DISAMBIGUATION: The 'Rouse' NO vote on the Jan 6 2020 VB 2A Constitutional City resolution (6-4) was AARON ROUSE (her husband, then at-large councilmember), NOT Jennifer Rouse. Jennifer Rouse did not take office until Jan 1 2023, three years after that vote. No public profession of Christ in capacity of office located.

jennifer-rouse.notes_set: [2026-06-04 19:19 RESOLUTE refinement] 30 LOCAL cells scored as 0T / 2F / 28 NULL = 0%. Strongest single signal: HARD FALSE public_justice q0 — on April 16, 2026 VB Council voted 10-1 to enact an emergency 9:30 p.m. all-ages Oceanfront curfew after TWO mass shootings; Jennifer Rouse was the LONE NO. She cited resident/worker income concerns and explicit pro-engagement framing: 'The community is active. We need, as a council and leadership, to be engaged with them.' After a judge issued a TRO blocking the curfew, she called it 'a lesson learned.' Opposite of affirmative police/public-safety advocacy — HIGH BAR clears the FALSE side via single most-prominent enforcement vote of her tenure. HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2 — voted YES on the May 13, 2025 FY26 budget (10-1; only Henley NO) raising meals tax 5.5%->6%, hiking stormwater + boat-reg fees, cutting library positions. NULL elsewhere — heavy public-position absence (no abortion, marriage, school choice, faith-profession, ICE, voter-ID statements located). CRITICAL DISAMBIGUATION REGISTERED: prior batch dossiers (06-04 18:30, 06-02 09:50) referenced 'Rouse' as a Jan 2020 NO vote — that was AARON Rouse (husband), NOT Jennifer Rouse. Jennifer's self_defense.1 is NULL not FALSE — she was not seated. HETERODOX CATCHES: (1) lone-NO curfew vote (single strongest 2026 public-justice signal — heterodox-D-leaning); (2) FY26 budget YES; (3) attended 'Hampton Roads Black Pride Gay Festival' as elected official (community-presence soft signal, NOT a vote — does not score biblical_marriage cells per discipline); (4) campaign-issues framing of public schools as 'our greatest asset' (institutional-investment, not parental-rights). Recommend revisit after Nov 2026 re-election outcome.

profile.background: Virginia Beach City Council, District 9 (Princess Anne / South VB; officially nonpartisan). NAME CORRECTION: legal name Joashua F. Schulman, goes by 'Joash' — slug 'joshua-schulman' retained for DB stability but display name updated. VPAP labels 'Independent Candidate'; NOT on RPVB roster; PVG-listed 2022. Solo real-estate / business attorney (Joashua F. Schulman, PC); co-founder + managing partner of Town Center Office Suites (160+ small businesses). Michigan State 2002 (Intl Relations); MSU College of Law 2005 JD. VB resident since 2007; Thoroughgood neighborhood; wife + 3 daughters. Civic boards: Chair VB Wetlands Board; VP VB Vision; Sec CBDA; Hampton Roads Chamber regional board; Lynnhaven River Now; Neptune Festival Foundation. Won D9 Nov 8, 2022 vs incumbent John Moss + TJ Morgan; sworn in Jan 1, 2023. Term ends Dec 31, 2026 — ON Nov 2026 ballot. 2022 endorsement coalition: PBA, VBEA-PACE (teachers union), VB Professional Fire & EMS Local 2924, Hampton Roads Chamber, DTRT PAC. No public profession of Christian faith located in capacity of office.

profile.background: Virginia Beach City Council, District 3 (officially nonpartisan). Runs as Independent on ballot but RPVB-endorsed (RPVB City Committee 2024 endorsement; appears on rpvb.org 'My Elected Representatives' page) — heterodox-R lane. First openly gay member of VB City Council. CEO of Arts Alliance (regional arts nonprofit); former Community Engagement Manager at Chrysler Museum of Art. First Colonial HS; George Mason University Government grad; Sorensen + Hunt Institute fellowships. Initially appointed May 7, 2019 by 9-1 Council vote to fill SHANNON KANE's vacated Rose Hall seat (CROSS-BATCH CORRECTION: not Sessoms's seat); won special election Nov 5, 2019; re-elected 2020; redistricted into D3 with 10-1 transition; re-elected Nov 5, 2024 (50.53% vs Cleon Jones 31.78% + Christina Felder 17.05%). Current 4-yr D3 term ends Dec 31, 2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. Former president Hampton Roads Pride; 2018 Equality Virginia 'OUTstanding Virginian'; co-chair VB For Fairness (LGBTQ lobby); VB Human Rights Commission member; appointed by BOTH Gov. Northam (D) AND Gov. Youngkin (R) to Virginia LGBTQ+ Advisory Board. 2024 endorsements: VB PBA 34, Thin Blue Line VA, FOP Lodge #8, Sheriff Rocky Holcomb, former Chief Jake Jacocks, RPVB City Committee. No public profession of Christian faith located in capacity of office.

michael-berlucchi.notes_set: [2026-06-04 19:19 RESOLUTE refinement] STANDOUT SPLIT-ISSUE RECORD. 30 LOCAL cells scored as 2T / 4F / 24 NULL = 33% (6 scored cells). HARD TRUE self_defense q1 — voted YES Jan 6, 2020 on the 6-4 VB 'Second Amendment Constitutional City' resolution (NO: Henley, Jones, Aaron Rouse, Wooten; YES: Berlucchi, Dyer, Wood, Moss, Abbott, Wilson) — appointed to Council May 2019, seated for the vote. HARD TRUE public_justice q0 — affirmative public-safety advocacy documented + 2024 endorsements from VB PBA 34, FOP Lodge #8, Thin Blue Line VA, Sheriff Holcomb, ex-Chief Jacocks ('Michael Berlucchi has been an ardent supporter of public safety...ensuring the Police Department has innovative technology and training'); campaign cites academy funding + officer compensation. Clears HIGH BAR for affirmative advocacy. HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q0 — openly gay, former Hampton Roads Pride president, co-chair VB For Fairness LGBTQ advocacy; participated in 2020 city LGBT Pride proclamation. HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q2 — sponsored October 2019 Council legislative request asking GA to BAN CONVERSION THERAPY for sexual orientation AND gender identity; dual-governor LGBTQ+ Advisory Board appointee; publicly defended trans individuals in 2019 HRC controversy ('have hurt my heart'). HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q4 — active backer of city LGBT Pride proclamations (June 2020+; entire public identity); does NOT oppose Pride flag/proclamations. HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2 — voted YES with 10-1 FY26 majority on the meals-tax/stormwater/boat-reg/library-cut budget despite 'keeping taxes low' campaign rhetoric. NULL elsewhere. HETERODOX CATCHES (multiple, as expected): (1) Triple HARD-FALSE biblical_marriage cluster — strongest LGBT-advocacy record in VBCC + only councilmember to AFFIRMATIVELY sponsor an LGBTQ-protective Council legislative request (Oct 2019 conversion-therapy ban); (2) Economic FALSE despite low-tax rhetoric (FY26 YES); (3) Classic split-issue R-aligned-but-socially-progressive: pro-cop + pro-2A + pro-LGBT + pro-tax-hike; (4) Party-label asymmetry — runs Independent but RPVB-endorsed; db 'R' is defensible per RPVB precedent; (5) CROSS-BATCH CORRECTION — Berlucchi was appointed May 7 2019 to fill Shannon Kane's Rose Hall seat, NOT Sessoms's (Sessoms resigned mayoralty 2018, succeeded by Dyer per 18:30 batch dossier). Recommend revisit after any 2026 Council action on LGBT items or sanctuary policy.

profile.background: Virginia Beach Vice Mayor + City Council District 5 (officially nonpartisan; RPVB-endorsed). Rosemary Capps Wilson (b. ~1951). Bayside HS; summa cum laude Old Dominion University BS (education); former public school teacher; long-time Howard Hanna realtor (VB office). VB School Board 1996-2000 (Vice-Chair); first elected to City Council Nov 2000, seated Jan 2001. Redistricted into District 5 with 2022/2024 10-1 transition. First became Vice Mayor in 2021 (filling Jim Wood's resignation); reappointed Jan 2023; reappointed Jan 7, 2025 on 10-vote majority. Current D5 term ends Dec 31, 2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. ~30 years total elected service (School Board + Council) makes her one of the most senior elected officials in Hampton Roads. CRITICAL PROMPT CORRECTION: She is NOT married to former Mayor Will Sessoms — she is a widow whose husband died of a rare cancer ~2017-2019. She founded a personal cancer-research fundraising effort that raised $1.2M+. Two adult daughters: Kari (MBA Northwestern) + Sarah (MPP Columbia). Endorsement profile: RPVB + George Allen + Ken Stolle + Tim Anderson (all R) — plus historically some D state senators per WAVY 2024 profile (consensus-incumbent posture). No specific church affiliation or public profession of Christian faith located in capacity of office.

rosemary-wilson.notes_set: [2026-06-04 19:19 RESOLUTE refinement] 30 LOCAL cells scored as 1T / 2F / 27 NULL = 33% (3 scored cells). HARD TRUE self_defense q1 — voted YES Jan 6 2020 on 6-4 VB 'Second Amendment Constitutional City' resolution; on Council since 2001, was NOT in the NO bloc (Henley + Jones + Aaron Rouse + Wooten NO), therefore YES with prevailing 6-member majority (Dyer/Wilson/Wood/Moss/Abbott/Berlucchi). HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2 — voted YES with 10-1 majority on May 13 2025 FY26 budget (meals tax 5.5%->6%, stormwater + boat-reg fees, library cuts; only Henley NO); also publicly entertained a per-bedroom short-term-rental fee (~$1.7M revenue) in Jan 2026, saying the city 'would need to know how the additional revenue might be used.' SOFT FALSE biblical_marriage q4 — as Vice Mayor presiding in Mayor Dyer's absence over a Council that has issued LGBT Pride proclamations annually since June 2020; NO recorded dissent across 5+ consecutive years of Pride proclamations despite RPVB endorsement. SOFT due to acquiescence-without-action (no overt promotion, no overt opposition) — but multi-year non-objection while in executive Vice-Mayor capacity meets discipline threshold. NULL EVERYWHERE ELSE. No statement on abortion, 1M-1W marriage, trans/SOGI, parental rights, school choice, faith profession, ICE cooperation, voter ID, COVID, sanctuary. HETERODOX CATCHES: (1) CRITICAL PROMPT CORRECTION REGISTERED — Wilson is NOT Sessoms's wife; she is a widow (husband died of rare cancer ~2017-2019); $1.2M cancer-research legacy in his memory; the prompt's hypothesis was wrong and is flagged across batches; (2) Strong VB-2A 2020 YES coexists with strong FY26 fiscal YES — votes with city manager on revenue; (3) Consensus-incumbent endorsement profile (RPVB + some D state senators per WAVY profile) — establishment R-bipartisan; (4) Five-plus consecutive years of council acquiescence to Pride proclamations as Vice Mayor; (5) Long service ~30 yrs (School Board 1996-2000 + Council 2001-present); successor to Jim Wood as Vice Mayor 2021. Recommend revisit after any 2026-2028 dissent on a specific proclamation or budget vote.
